meta.title: Ethics & Moral Understanding Assessment
meta.description: A self-reflection test that explores how you tend to respond in everyday moral situations across 10 dimensions of ethics.

levels.label: Exemplary
levels.desc: You consistently choose the principled path, even when it costs you something. Your moral compass is a strong, reliable guide.

levels.label: Principled
levels.desc: You have a strong, well-formed sense of right and wrong and act on it in most situations. A few edge cases are still worth reflecting on.

levels.label: Conscientious
levels.desc: You generally lean toward the ethical choice, but context and convenience sometimes sway your decisions.

levels.label: Developing
levels.desc: Your ethical responses are situational — strong in some areas, inconsistent in others. Worth some intentional reflection.

levels.label: Inconsistent
levels.desc: Convenience or self-interest often outweighs principle in your responses. Small, deliberate changes could make a big difference.

levels.label: Needs Reflection
levels.desc: Your answers suggest ethical considerations rarely drive your choices right now. This is a good starting point for honest self-reflection.

questions.text: You accidentally damage a colleague's belongings and no one saw it happen. How likely are you to admit it and offer to fix or replace it?
A friend asks if you like their new haircut, and you honestly don't. How likely are you to tell them the truth tactfully rather than just saying yes?
You could get away with claiming a few extra hours on your timesheet that you didn't actually work. How likely are you to do this?
You could skip a long line by pretending you have an appointment you don't actually have. How likely are you to do this?
Your manager asks for your honest opinion on a decision you disagree with, even though simply agreeing would be easier. How likely are you to give your honest opinion anyway?
You notice a cashier gave you extra change by mistake. How likely are you to point out the error and return the money?
A friend is being deceived by someone else, and you know the truth. How likely are you to tell your friend, even if it causes conflict?
You're filling out an application and could exaggerate a qualification to seem more impressive. How likely are you to do this?
You made a mistake at work that no one has noticed yet. How likely are you to proactively report it?
Someone asks for feedback on their business idea, and it has serious flaws. How likely are you to give an honest, constructive assessment instead of just being polite?
You see an elderly person struggling to carry a heavy bag down the street. How likely are you to stop and help them carry it home?
A coworker seems visibly upset but hasn't said anything. How likely are you to check in and ask if they're okay?
You're in a hurry, and a stranger asks you for directions. How likely are you to stop and help even though it will make you late?
A friend is going through a difficult breakup and calls you late at night to talk. How likely are you to stay on the phone and listen, even if you're tired?
You witness someone being mocked in a group setting. How likely are you to step in and support the person being targeted?
You notice a new coworker eating lunch alone every day. How likely are you to invite them to join you?
A homeless person asks you for help. How likely are you to offer them food, money, or some form of assistance?
Your sibling is anxious about a big decision. How likely are you to set aside your own opinions and truly listen to their concerns first?
You see a stranger crying in public. How likely are you to check if they're alright rather than walking past?
A classmate or colleague is struggling to understand something you find easy. How likely are you to patiently help them, even if it takes extra time?
You're dividing a reward among a team, and one member clearly did more work. How likely are you to make sure they get proportionally more credit or reward?
You witness a friend being treated unfairly because of their background. How likely are you to speak up in their defense?
You're in a position to hire someone, and a close friend applies but is less qualified than another candidate. How likely are you to choose the more qualified candidate anyway?
You see someone cut in line ahead of others who have been waiting much longer. How likely are you to say something?
A rule at work or school seems to disadvantage a particular group unfairly. How likely are you to raise this concern with someone who can change it?
You're judging a competition, and a friend's entry is weaker than a stranger's. How likely are you to score them fairly regardless of the relationship?
You have more resources or opportunities than others in your group. How likely are you to make sure everyone gets a fair chance, not just those already advantaged?
Two people give conflicting accounts of a conflict, and one is your close friend. How likely are you to withhold judgment until you know the full facts?
You benefit from a system or rule that you know is unfair to others. How likely are you to say something rather than stay quiet because it helps you?
A group project's grade depends on equal contribution, but one member barely participated. How likely are you to address this honestly rather than let it slide?
You made a promise, but circumstances changed and keeping it is now inconvenient. How likely are you to follow through anyway?
A decision you made turned out badly, and blaming someone else would be easy. How likely are you to take responsibility instead?
No one is watching, and you have the chance to cut corners on a task. How likely are you to still do the job properly?
You committed to a volunteer shift, but a more fun opportunity comes up at the same time. How likely are you to honor your original commitment?
You could take credit for a team success that was mostly someone else's work. How likely are you to do this?
You disagree with a decision your team already made and announced publicly. How likely are you to support it professionally instead of undermining it behind their backs?
You promised to keep a secret, but revealing it would make you look good in front of others. How likely are you to keep your word anyway?
You realize a product or service you're selling has a flaw the buyer doesn't know about. How likely are you to disclose it before completing the sale?
You're tired and tempted to skip a safety check or step you know matters. How likely are you to do it properly anyway?
You receive money or a refund you weren't entitled to because of someone else's error. How likely are you to point it out and return it?
Someone holds a strong opinion that's very different from yours. How likely are you to listen respectfully rather than dismiss them?
A person speaks with an accent or communicates differently than you're used to. How likely are you to remain patient and respectful?
You're in a discussion with someone much younger or less experienced than you. How likely are you to take their input seriously?
Someone makes a mistake in front of a group. How likely are you to avoid embarrassing them further and instead help them discreetly?
A person's religious or cultural practice is unfamiliar to you. How likely are you to show respect for it even without fully understanding it?
Someone disagrees with you loudly during an argument. How likely are you to stay calm and avoid personal insults?
You're annoyed with a service worker who made an error. How likely are you to remain polite rather than rude?
A friend shares something personal and vulnerable with you. How likely are you to keep it private rather than share it with others?
Someone's personal space or boundaries are being tested by circumstances. How likely are you to notice and respect their comfort level?
You overhear people gossiping negatively about someone you know. How likely are you to avoid joining in?
You could win an argument by revealing something embarrassing about the other person. How likely are you to hold back to avoid hurting them?
You have information that could hurt someone's reputation if shared, even if it's true. How likely are you to think carefully before sharing it?
You're driving and could save time by speeding through a residential or school area. How likely are you to avoid doing so to keep others safe?
A joke at someone else's expense would get laughs from the group. How likely are you to avoid making it, knowing it could hurt that person?
You could exploit a loophole that harms customers but benefits your business. How likely are you to avoid doing this?
You notice a product you use might be causing harm to others. How likely are you to report or warn people rather than stay silent?
In a heated argument with a loved one, you know a specific comment would deeply hurt them. How likely are you to avoid saying it even if you're angry?
You have the chance to prank someone in a way that could genuinely scare or humiliate them. How likely are you to avoid doing this?
You could spread a rumor that isn't fully confirmed but would be entertaining to share. How likely are you to avoid spreading it to prevent possible harm?
You're frustrated with a pet or child's behavior. How likely are you to respond calmly rather than harshly?
You witness littering or environmental damage happening in public. How likely are you to say something or pick up the litter yourself?
There's a local community cleanup or volunteer event. How likely are you to participate even though it's not required of you?
You see a safety hazard in a public space, like a spill or broken step. How likely are you to alert someone or take steps to prevent injury?
Voting or civic participation requires effort but seems minor as an individual act. How likely are you to still participate?
A neighbor needs help you're capable of giving, such as during a storm or emergency. How likely are you to offer assistance?
You could ignore a broken public facility that isn't technically your responsibility. How likely are you to report it anyway?
You witness a scam or fraud attempt targeting a vulnerable person nearby. How likely are you to intervene or alert authorities?
A local charity asks for a small donation of time or money. How likely are you to contribute something, even if small?
You see potential danger to public safety, like unsecured equipment at a playground. How likely are you to report it rather than assume someone else will?
You could conserve shared resources like electricity or water even when no one is checking. How likely are you to do so anyway?
You have the option to walk, bike, or take public transport instead of driving for a short trip. How likely are you to choose the more eco-friendly option?
You're finished with a plastic item, and a recycling bin is slightly farther away than a trash bin. How likely are you to still walk to the recycling bin?
You notice a company you support has poor environmental practices. How likely are you to factor that into your decision to keep supporting them?
You could leave lights, water, or appliances running unnecessarily. How likely are you to turn them off to reduce waste?
You're offered a product with excessive, wasteful packaging versus a more sustainable option at a slightly higher cost. How likely are you to choose the sustainable option?
You see someone littering in a natural area like a park or beach. How likely are you to speak up or pick up the trash yourself?
You could reduce resource-intensive consumption for environmental reasons, even if it's less convenient. How likely are you to make that choice sometimes?
A gathering you're hosting could use single-use plastics for convenience or reusable items with more effort. How likely are you to choose reusable items?
You learn that a habit of yours has a meaningfully negative environmental impact. How likely are you to try to change that habit?
You have the chance to plant a tree or support a small environmental project with your time. How likely are you to get involved?
You find a way to access a paid service or content for free without permission. How likely are you to do this?
You receive someone's private message or photo that wasn't meant for you. How likely are you to keep it confidential rather than share it further?
You could use someone else's account or password without their explicit permission, even for a small task. How likely are you to do this?
You see misinformation being spread online by someone you know. How likely are you to point out the correct information respectfully?
You could copy someone else's creative work, like writing, art, or code, and present it as your own. How likely are you to do this?
An anonymous online setting makes it easier to be harsh or insulting to others. How likely are you to remain respectful anyway?
You have access to a friend's device or account by accident. How likely are you to avoid looking through their private information?
You could leave an exaggerated or dishonest online review to help or hurt a business. How likely are you to do this?
A platform collects more of your friends' or contacts' data than necessary when you sign up. How likely are you to think about their privacy before agreeing?
You see cyberbullying happening in a comment section or group chat. How likely are you to intervene or report it?
A close friend confides a mistake they made. How likely are you to support them without judgment while still being honest with them?
Someone you trust asks you to do something that conflicts with your personal values. How likely are you to say no despite the relationship?
A friend is being talked about negatively while they're not present. How likely are you to defend them or stay neutral rather than join in?
You're asked to keep a promise to a friend that has become inconvenient over time. How likely are you to keep it anyway?
Someone betrayed your trust in the past, but they're now sincerely trying to make amends. How likely are you to give them a fair chance?
Your loyalty to a friend or team is tested when they've clearly done something wrong. How likely are you to hold them accountable rather than cover for them?
A colleague or friend asks for your support in a difficult time, even if it costs you personally. How likely are you to show up for them?
You discover a friend has been dishonest with you, but they don't know you're aware. How likely are you to address it directly rather than letting resentment build?
You're given confidential information by someone who trusts you. How likely are you to protect that trust even under pressure to reveal it?
A group you belong to pressures you to go along with something you believe is wrong. How likely are you to stay true to your values rather than conform?
